name: OriginLab Origin
description: A data analysis and graphing software widely used in experimental physics labs for creating publication-quality figures with sophisticated curve fitting.
whyUseIt: Origin provides an Excel-like interface with powerful curve fitting, peak analysis, and statistical tools built specifically for scientific data. It produces camera-ready figures that meet journal requirements. Many experimentalists prefer it for its ease of use.
fieldPosition: Experimental physics — data analysis and publication-quality graphing
physicistWorkflow: Import data, plot it, fit a model, customise the figure for publication, and export.
supervisorExpects: Professional-quality figures with proper axis labels, units, error bars, and legends.
computationalImpact: Streamlines the path from raw data to publication-ready figures.

vocabulary.definition: A spreadsheet-like data table in Origin
Fitting arbitrary mathematical models to data using iterative methods

beginnerMistakes: Not including error bars on data points
Using default plot styles instead of customising for publication
